question: Show me the upcoming appointments.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time >= NOW()
ORDER BY a.schedule_time ASC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;
generated_final_answer:
Total matching records: 2

question: List the completed calls.
notes: Past appointment count uses schedule_time < NOW(); attended/completed non-no-show uses no_show = false.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.no_show = false
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Which calls already happened?
notes: Past appointment count uses schedule_time < NOW(); attended/completed non-no-show uses no_show = false.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Show me attended calls only.
notes: Past appointment count uses schedule_time < NOW(); attended/completed non-no-show uses no_show = false.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.no_show = false
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

    l.first_name,

  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,

  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1

    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id

question: Show call history for the appointment records.
notes: Past appointment count uses schedule_time < NOW(); attended/completed non-no-show uses no_show = false.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  f.id AS fathom_record_id,
  a.id AS appointment_id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(f.ai_generated_title), ''), NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), 'Untitled Call') AS call_title,
  f.summary,
  f.key_points,
  f.action_items,
  f.objections,
  f.call_duration_seconds,
  f.call_started_at,
  f.call_ended_at
FROM fathom_call_records f
JOIN appointments a
  ON a.id = f.appointment_id
 AND a.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id
 AND a.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
WHERE f.clerk_org_id = :org_id
ORDER BY COALESCE(f.call_started_at, a.schedule_time, f.created_at) DESC, f.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: List the last 20 completed appointments.
notes: Past appointment count uses schedule_time < NOW(); attended/completed non-no-show uses no_show = false.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.no_show = false
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Which completed calls have Fathom records?
notes: Past appointment count uses schedule_time < NOW(); attended/completed non-no-show uses no_show = false.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  true AS has_fathom_record,
  f.id AS fathom_record_id,
  f.call_started_at,
  f.call_duration_seconds
FROM appointments a
JOIN fathom_call_records f
  ON f.appointment_id = a.id
 AND f.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.no_show = false
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC, f.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Which past appointments do not have Fathom attached?
notes: Past appointment count uses schedule_time < NOW(); attended/completed non-no-show uses no_show = false.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  false AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  )
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Which event type has the highest no-show rate?
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  COUNT(*) AS total_past_appointments,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE a.no_show = true) AS no_show_appointments,
  ROUND(
    100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE a.no_show = true) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0),
    2
  ) AS no_show_rate_percent,
  SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER() AS total_matching_past_appointments,
  ROUND(COUNT(*) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER(), 0), 2) AS percentage_of_total_past_appointments
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
GROUP BY COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type')

question: Which event type had the most missed calls last week?

WITH last_week_no_shows AS (
  SELECT
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
    COUNT(*) AS missed_calls,
    SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER() AS total_matching_appointments
  FROM appointments a
  LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
    ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
   AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
   AND aet.is_deleted = false
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
    AND a.no_show = true
    AND a.schedule_time >= DATE_TRUNC('week', NOW()) - INTERVAL '1 week'
    AND a.schedule_time < DATE_TRUNC('week', NOW())
    AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  GROUP BY COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type')
), ranked AS (
  SELECT
    event_name,
    missed_calls,
    total_matching_appointments,
    DENSE_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY missed_calls DESC, event_name ASC) AS rnk
  FROM last_week_no_shows
)
SELECT
  event_name,
  missed_calls,
  total_matching_appointments
FROM ranked
WHERE rnk = 1
ORDER BY event_name ASC;

generated_sql:
SELECT
  COALESCE(CAST(a.source AS text), 'UNKNOWN') AS appointment_source,
  COUNT(*) AS total_past_appointments,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE a.no_show = true) AS no_show_appointments,
  ROUND(
    100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE a.no_show = true) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0),
    2
  ) AS no_show_rate_percent,
  SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER() AS total_matching_past_appointments,
  ROUND(COUNT(*) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER(), 0), 2) AS percentage_of_total_past_appointments
FROM appointments a
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
GROUP BY COALESCE(CAST(a.source AS text), 'UNKNOWN')
ORDER BY no_show_rate_percent DESC NULLS LAST, total_past_appointments DESC, appointment_source ASC;
generated_final_answer:
Total past appointments: 449

Here is the no-show breakdown by source:

| Source | Past appointments | No-shows | No-show % | % of total past appointments |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Calendly | 449 | 54 | 12.03% | 100.00% |

question: Which host has the highest no-show rate?
generated_sql:
WITH host_no_show_rates AS (
  SELECT
    NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), '') AS host_id,
    COUNT(*) AS total_past_appointments,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE a.no_show = true) AS no_show_appointments,
    ROUND(
      100.0 * COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE a.no_show = true) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0),
      2
    ) AS no_show_rate_percent
  FROM appointments a
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
    AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
    AND NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), '') IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), '')
),
max_rate AS (
  SELECT MAX(no_show_rate_percent) AS max_no_show_rate_percent
  FROM host_no_show_rates
)
SELECT
  hnr.host_id,
  hnr.total_past_appointments,
  hnr.no_show_appointments,
  hnr.no_show_rate_percent
FROM host_no_show_rates hnr
JOIN max_rate mr
  ON hnr.no_show_rate_percent = mr.max_no_show_rate_percent
ORDER BY hnr.no_show_appointments DESC, hnr.total_past_appointments DESC, hnr.host_id ASC;

question: What percentage of calls ended as won, lost, follow-up, or no-show?
generated_sql:
SELECT
  CASE
    WHEN a.no_show = true THEN 'NO_SHOW'
    WHEN outcome.role IN ('WON', 'LOST', 'FOLLOW_UP') THEN CAST(outcome.role AS text)
    ELSE 'OTHER'
  END AS outcome_role,
  COUNT(*) AS appointment_count,
  SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER() AS total_matching_appointments,
  ROUND(COUNT(*) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER(), 0), 2) AS percentage_of_total
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND (
    a.no_show = true
    OR outcome.role IN ('WON', 'LOST', 'FOLLOW_UP')
  )
GROUP BY
  CASE
    WHEN a.no_show = true THEN 'NO_SHOW'
    WHEN outcome.role IN ('WON', 'LOST', 'FOLLOW_UP') THEN CAST(outcome.role AS text)
    ELSE 'OTHER'
  END
ORDER BY appointment_count DESC, outcome_role ASC;

question: Which Calendly event had the most appointments last month?
generated_sql:
WITH last_month_appointments AS (
  SELECT
    COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
    COUNT(*) AS appointment_count
  FROM appointments a
  LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
    ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
   AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
   AND aet.is_deleted = false
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
    AND a.source = 'CALENDLY'
    AND a.schedule_time >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 month')
    AND a.schedule_time < DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)
  GROUP BY COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type')
), max_count AS (
  SELECT MAX(appointment_count) AS max_appointment_count
  FROM last_month_appointments
)
SELECT
  lma.event_name,
  lma.appointment_count
FROM last_month_appointments lma
JOIN max_count mc
  ON lma.appointment_count = mc.max_appointment_count
ORDER BY lma.event_name ASC;
generated_final_answer: Last month, the Calendly event with the most appointments was **Qualification Call Freedom Academy (TF)** with **178 appointments**.

question: Show appointments with missing host.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  'No Host' AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet

 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), '') IS NULL
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Which setter booked the most sales calls?
generated_sql:
WITH setter_counts AS (
  SELECT
    NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), '') AS setter_id,
    COUNT(*) AS appointment_count
  FROM appointments a
  WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
    AND a.is_deleted = false
    AND a.snapshot_call_category = 'SALES_CALL'
    AND NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), '') IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), '')
),
max_count AS (
  SELECT MAX(appointment_count) AS max_appointment_count
  FROM setter_counts
)
SELECT
  sc.setter_id,
  sc.appointment_count
FROM setter_counts sc
JOIN max_count mc
  ON sc.appointment_count = mc.max_appointment_count
ORDER BY sc.setter_id ASC;

    AND a.no_show = false

question: Show me all appointments.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: List appointments with lead names.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Show me calls with outcome and host.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: List appointments scheduled this month.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)
  AND a.schedule_time < DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) + INTERVAL '1 month'
ORDER BY a.schedule_time ASC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time >= :start_date
  AND a.schedule_time < :end_date
ORDER BY a.schedule_time ASC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Give me appointments sorted by latest schedule time.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: List sales call appointments only.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.snapshot_call_category = 'SALES_CALL'
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.snapshot_call_category = 'COACHING_CALL'
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.source = 'MANUAL'
ORDER BY a.created_at DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Which appointments ended as Won?
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') = 'WON'
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Which appointments ended as Lost?
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') = 'LOST'
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Show appointments that have no outcome label.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  'No Outcome' AS outcome_name,
  'NO_OUTCOME' AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.outcome_id IS NULL
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;
generated_final_answer: There are no appointments without an outcome label.

question: List appointments with their Fathom status.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CASE
    WHEN EXISTS (
      SELECT 1
      FROM fathom_call_records f_check
      WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
        AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
    ) THEN 'Has Fathom Record'
    ELSE 'No Fathom Record'
  END AS fathom_status
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Which appointments were no-shows?
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.no_show = true
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Show me the no-show appointment list.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.no_show = true
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: List calls where the lead did not show up.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.no_show = true
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Which scheduled calls were missed?
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.no_show = true
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Show no-show calls from this month.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)
  AND a.schedule_time < LEAST(NOW(), DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) + INTERVAL '1 month')
  AND a.no_show = true
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;
generated_final_answer: There are no no-show calls scheduled so far this month.

question: Give me no-show appointments with host and setter.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.no_show = true
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: List the latest no-show appointments.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.no_show = true
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Which Calendly appointments were no-shows?
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.no_show = true
  AND a.source = 'CALENDLY'
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Which manual appointments were no-shows?
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.no_show = true
  AND a.source = 'MANUAL'
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;
generated_final_answer: There are no manual appointments marked as no-shows.

question: List no-show coaching calls.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  ) AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.no_show = true
  AND a.snapshot_call_category = 'COACHING_CALL'
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;
generated_final_answer: There are no no-show coaching calls.

question: Which no-show appointments also have Fathom records?
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  true AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.no_show = true
  AND EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  )
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: What is the Fathom coverage for past appointments?
notes: For coverage, use past appointments by default; future appointments should not be counted as missing Fathom.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) AS total_past_appointments,
  COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) FILTER (WHERE f.id IS NOT NULL) AS appointments_with_fathom,
  COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) FILTER (WHERE f.id IS NULL) AS appointments_without_fathom,
  ROUND(
    100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) FILTER (WHERE f.id IS NOT NULL) / NULLIF(COUNT(DISTINCT a.id), 0),

LEFT JOIN fathom_call_records f

 AND f.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id

    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),

    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  false AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet

  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()

    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id

SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  false AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id

ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC

question: Which completed calls were not captured by Fathom?
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  false AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.no_show = false
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  )
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Show appointments missing Fathom for this month.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  false AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time >= DATE_TRUNC('month', NOW())
  AND a.schedule_time < LEAST(NOW(), DATE_TRUNC('month', NOW()) + INTERVAL '1 month')
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  )
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Which sales calls have no Fathom record?
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  false AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.snapshot_call_category = 'SALES_CALL'
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  )
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Which coaching calls are missing Fathom?
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  false AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.snapshot_call_category = 'COACHING_CALL'
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  )
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;
generated_final_answer: There are no past coaching calls missing Fathom.

  ROUND(
    COUNT(DISTINCT a.id) * 100.0 / NULLIF(SUM(COUNT(DISTINCT a.id)) OVER(), 0),

question: Show past Calendly calls with no Fathom record.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  false AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.source = 'CALENDLY'
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  )
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Show past manual calls with no Fathom record.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  false AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND a.source = 'MANUAL'
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  )
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;
generated_final_answer: There are no past manual calls without a Fathom record.

question: Give me the latest calls missing Fathom.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  false AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  )
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: List missed Fathom records for last week appointments.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  false AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time >= DATE_TRUNC('week', CURRENT_DATE) - INTERVAL '1 week'
  AND a.schedule_time < DATE_TRUNC('week', CURRENT_DATE)
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  )
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Show calls where Fathom was not matched.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  f.id AS fathom_record_id,
  f.fathom_call_id,
  f.fathom_meeting_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(f.ai_generated_title), ''), 'Untitled Call') AS call_title,
  f.summary,
  f.call_duration_seconds,
  f.call_started_at,
  f.call_ended_at,
  f.created_at
FROM fathom_call_records f
WHERE f.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND f.appointment_id IS NULL
ORDER BY COALESCE(f.call_started_at, f.created_at) DESC, f.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Which past calls are missing the Fathom link?
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  a.id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), aet.event_type_name, 'Unknown Event Type') AS event_name,
  CAST(a.snapshot_call_category AS text) AS call_category,
  COALESCE(outcome.name, 'No Outcome') AS outcome_name,
  COALESCE(CAST(outcome.role AS text), 'NO_OUTCOME') AS outcome_role,
  a.no_show,
  CAST(a.source AS text) AS appointment_source,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.host_id), ''), 'No Host') AS host_id,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(a.setter_id), ''), 'No Setter') AS setter_id,
  false AS has_fathom_record
FROM appointments a
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN sales_statuses outcome
  ON outcome.id = a.outcome_id
 AND outcome.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
LEFT JOIN appointment_event_types aet
  ON aet.id = a.appointment_event_type_id
 AND aet.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
 AND aet.is_deleted = false
WHERE a.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND a.is_deleted = false
  AND a.schedule_time < NOW()
  AND NOT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM fathom_call_records f_check
    WHERE f_check.appointment_id = a.id
      AND f_check.clerk_org_id = a.clerk_org_id
  )
ORDER BY a.schedule_time DESC, a.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Which appointments have Fathom summaries?
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  f.id AS fathom_record_id,
  a.id AS appointment_id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(f.ai_generated_title), ''), NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), 'Untitled Call') AS call_title,
  f.summary,
  f.call_duration_seconds,
  f.call_started_at,
  f.call_ended_at
FROM fathom_call_records f
JOIN appointments a
  ON a.id = f.appointment_id
 AND a.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id
 AND a.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
WHERE f.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND NULLIF(TRIM(f.summary), '') IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY COALESCE(f.call_started_at, a.schedule_time, f.created_at) DESC, f.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  f.id AS fathom_record_id,
  a.id AS appointment_id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(f.ai_generated_title), ''), NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), 'Untitled Call') AS call_title,
  f.summary,
  f.key_points,
  f.action_items,
  f.objections,
  f.call_duration_seconds,
  f.call_started_at,
  f.call_ended_at
FROM fathom_call_records f
JOIN appointments a
  ON a.id = f.appointment_id
 AND a.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id
 AND a.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
WHERE f.clerk_org_id = :org_id
ORDER BY COALESCE(f.call_started_at, a.schedule_time, f.created_at) DESC, f.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: List calls with Fathom summaries.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  f.id AS fathom_record_id,
  a.id AS appointment_id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(f.ai_generated_title), ''), NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), 'Untitled Call') AS call_title,
  f.summary,
  f.call_duration_seconds,
  f.call_started_at,
  f.call_ended_at
FROM fathom_call_records f
JOIN appointments a
  ON a.id = f.appointment_id
 AND a.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id
 AND a.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
WHERE f.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND NULLIF(TRIM(f.summary), '') IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY COALESCE(f.call_started_at, a.schedule_time, f.created_at) DESC, f.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

question: Show recent Fathom summaries with lead names.
generated_sql:
SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  f.id AS fathom_record_id,
  a.id AS appointment_id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(f.ai_generated_title), ''), NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), 'Untitled Call') AS call_title,
  f.summary,
  f.call_duration_seconds,
  f.call_started_at,
  f.call_ended_at
FROM fathom_call_records f
JOIN appointments a
  ON a.id = f.appointment_id
 AND a.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id
 AND a.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
WHERE f.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND NULLIF(TRIM(f.summary), '') IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY COALESCE(f.call_started_at, a.schedule_time, f.created_at) DESC, f.id ASC
LIMIT 20;

SELECT
  COUNT(*) OVER() AS total_matching_rows,
  f.id AS fathom_record_id,
  a.id AS appointment_id,
  a.schedule_time,
  COALESCE(
    NULLIF(TRIM(l.full_name), ''),
    NULLIF(TRIM(CONCAT_WS(' ', l.first_name, l.last_name)), ''),
    l.first_name,
    'Unknown Lead'
  ) AS display_name,
  COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(f.ai_generated_title), ''), NULLIF(TRIM(a.snapshot_event_name), ''), 'Untitled Call') AS call_title,
  f.ai_generated_title,
  f.call_duration_seconds,
  f.call_started_at,
  f.call_ended_at
FROM fathom_call_records f
JOIN appointments a
  ON a.id = f.appointment_id
 AND a.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id
 AND a.is_deleted = false
LEFT JOIN leads l
  ON l.id = a.lead_id
 AND l.clerk_org_id = f.clerk_org_id
 AND l.is_deleted = false
WHERE f.clerk_org_id = :org_id
  AND NULLIF(TRIM(f.ai_generated_title), '') IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY COALESCE(f.call_started_at, a.schedule_time, f.created_at) DESC, f.id ASC
LIMIT 20;
